description_long: This house is an elevated structure supported by multiple slender columns, typical of stilt houses found in tropical or flood-prone regions. The roof is a prominent, steeply pitched gable style, covered with dark shingles and featuring a central ridge. The main living area is raised above ground level, accessible by a central staircase leading to an open entryway. The house appears to have open sides or large windows, allowing for ventilation and visibility. The materials suggest a combination of wood for the structural frame and columns, with a metal or shingle roof. The overall design is functional, with a focus on protection from ground moisture and flooding.
description: A stilted house with a steep gable roof, raised above ground on columns and accessed by a central staircase. The structure features open sides and a dark, shingled roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central staircase leading up to the open entryway, with the gable roof and supporting columns clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the roof and the arrangement of supporting columns beneath the elevated living area.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a visible staircase and open entryway, emphasizing the symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the roof's expanse and the stilted support system.
attribute_window_arrangement: open sides or large window openings
attribute_entryway_design: Central staircase leading to an open entryway, no porch, with a simple overhang formed by the roof
